argument_substitution.mechanism: Workflow markdown files are plain prompt templates with no documented argument-substitution placeholder (no {{args}} or equivalent)

builtin_commands.mechanism: Built-in slash commands (e.g., /newtask, /smol alias /compact, /newrule, /deep-planning, /explain-changes in VS Code, /reportbug) are hardcoded and not user-modifiable; user-authored workflows are additive, invoked alongside built-ins

providerExtensions.name: Filename as Command Name (Workflows)
providerExtensions.summary: Markdown filenames under the workflows directory become the invocation trigger; users type /<filename>.md including the .md extension in chat.

providerExtensions.name: Two-Scope Workflow Loading (Project and Global)
providerExtensions.summary: Project workflows in .clinerules/workflows/ and global workflows in ~/Documents/Cline/Workflows/ are both discovered; project scope wins on name collision.

providerExtensions.name: Plain Markdown Instruction Body (No Frontmatter)
providerExtensions.summary: Workflow files have no documented frontmatter schema; the entire markdown body is instruction text for the agent to execute as a multi-step workflow.

providerExtensions.name: Built-In Slash Commands
providerExtensions.summary: Hardcoded slash commands including /newtask, /smol (alias /compact), /newrule, /deep-planning, /explain-changes (VS Code only), /reportbug; separate from user-authored workflows.

providerExtensions.name: /newtask Context Distillation for Long Sessions
providerExtensions.summary: Built-in /newtask command AI-distills the current conversation's plan, files, and next steps into a fresh task with clean context.

providerExtensions.name: /deep-planning Four-Phase Implementation Planning
providerExtensions.summary: Built-in /deep-planning command runs investigate -> clarify -> plan -> task phases and writes implementation_plan.md to the workspace.
